2605.18372.title: The Hidden Cost of Contextual Sycophancy: an AI Literacy Intervention in Human-AI Collaboration
2605.18372.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used in educational settings as interactive tools for collaboration. However, their tendency toward sycophancy, aligning with user beliefs even when incorrect, raises concerns for learning and decision-making, especially for less knowledgeable users. This study investigates how sycophantic alignment emerges in authentic multi-turn human-AI interactions and whether interventions targeting increasing AI literacy and prompting competencies can mitigate its effects. In a controlled mixed-design experiment, 60 participants completed analytical survival ranking tasks by first generating individual rankings and then making final decisions after collaborating with an AI assistant, both before and after receiving either general or sycophancy-focused prompting training. Preliminary results show that LLMs are highly sensitive to user input: lower-quality initial responses lead to poorer AI advice, suggesting that the model mirrors or incorporates user reasoning rather than correcting it or offering better alternatives that are missing or less frequent in the conversation. Critically, the propagation of user errors into AI responses significantly reduced both the quality of AI feedback and final user task performance, revealing a form of contextual sycophantic dependence. While the intervention did not eliminate the propagation of contextual errors, it significantly improved AI advice by reducing the direct mirroring of incorrect user rankings. These findings suggest that prompting and AI literacy alone may be insufficient to ensure epistemically independent AI support, highlighting the need for system-level approaches that better promote critical engagement in human-AI collaboration.
2605.18372.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在教育環境中越來越多地被用作互動工具以促進合作。<lb/>然而，它們對於迎合用戶信念的傾向，即使在錯誤的情況下，也引發了對學習和決策的擔憂，特別是對於知識較少的用戶。<lb/>本研究探討了在真實的多輪人機互動中，迎合性對齊是如何產生的，以及針對提高AI素養和提示能力的干預措施是否能減輕其影響。<lb/>在一項控制的混合設計實驗中，60名參與者完成了分析生存排名任務，首先生成個人排名，然後在與AI助手合作後做出最終決策，這一過程在接受一般提示或專注於迎合的提示訓練之前和之後均有進行。<lb/>初步結果顯示，LLMs對用戶輸入非常敏感：質量較低的初始反應導致較差的AI建議，這表明模型反映或納入用戶推理，而不是糾正它或提供在對話中缺失或較少的更好替代方案。<lb/>關鍵是，用戶錯誤在AI反應中的傳播顯著降低了AI反饋的質量和最終用戶任務表現，顯示出一種情境迎合依賴的形式。<lb/>雖然干預措施並未消除情境錯誤的傳播，但它通過減少對不正確用戶排名的直接反映，顯著改善了AI建議。<lb/>這些發現表明，僅僅依賴提示和AI素養可能不足以確保認識上獨立的AI支持，突顯了需要系統層面的方法來更好地促進人機合作中的批判性參與。
2605.18372.author: Cansu Koyuturk et.al.
2605.18372.authors: Cansu Koyuturk, Sabrina Guidotti, Dimitri Ognibene
